name: George Town
description: A living museum of Straits trading culture — clan jetties, shophouses, temples and street art across a UNESCO-listed old town.

name: Historic Melaka
description: Five centuries of Malay, Portuguese, Dutch and British rule layered into one riverside city — the Stadthuys, A Famosa and Jonker Street.

name: Kinabalu Park
description: Malaysia's first UNESCO site, centred on 4,095 m Mount Kinabalu — a biodiversity hotspot with thousands of plant species.

name: Gunung Mulu National Park
description: Vast limestone caves, razor-sharp karst pinnacles and dense Borneo rainforest — home to the world's largest cave chamber.

name: Archaeological Heritage of Lenggong Valley
description: Nearly two million years of human history, including the 11,000-year-old 'Perak Man', Malaysia's oldest complete skeleton.

name: Cheong Fatt Tze Mansion
description: The indigo-blue courtyard mansion of a 19th-century tycoon, a multi-award-winning example of Hakka–Teochew eclectic architecture.

name: Fort Cornwallis
description: Malaysia's largest standing fort, built by the British East India Company in 1786 on the spot where Francis Light first landed.

name: Kek Lok Si Temple
description: Southeast Asia's largest Buddhist temple, rising in tiers up Air Itam hill to a 30 m bronze statue of Kuan Yin.

name: A Famosa (Porta de Santiago)
description: The surviving gatehouse of a 1511 Portuguese fortress — one of the oldest European architectural remains in Southeast Asia.

name: The Stadthuys & Dutch Square
description: The salmon-red 1650 Dutch town hall and Christ Church — the oldest surviving Dutch architecture in the East.

name: Jonker Street
description: Melaka's antique-and-food artery in the heart of Chinatown, transforming into a buzzing night market each weekend.

name: Sultan Abdul Samad Building
description: A copper-domed 1897 Moorish landmark facing Merdeka Square, where Malaya's flag of independence first flew in 1957.

name: Merdeka Square
description: The green padang where independence was declared at midnight on 31 August 1957, ringed by colonial-era landmarks.

name: Masjid Jamek
description: KL's oldest mosque (1909), an Indo-Saracenic landmark at the very confluence of rivers that gave the city its name.

name: Thean Hou Temple
description: A six-tiered Chinese temple to the Heavenly Mother, blending Buddhist, Confucian and Taoist worship under sweeping pagoda roofs.

name: Sri Mahamariamman Temple
description: KL's oldest Hindu temple (1873), its gopuram tower crowded with brightly painted deities at the edge of Chinatown.

name: Batu Caves
description: A 400-million-year-old limestone cave temple behind a 42.7 m golden statue of Murugan and a rainbow flight of 272 steps.

name: Sultan Salahuddin Abdul Aziz Mosque
description: The 'Blue Mosque' of Shah Alam — one of the world's largest, crowned by a vast blue-and-silver dome and four soaring minarets.

name: Putra Mosque
description: The rose-tinted granite mosque on Putrajaya's lakeside, its design drawing on Persian and Malay-Islamic tradition.

name: Sarawak Cultural Village
description: A living museum at the foot of Mount Santubong where Borneo's peoples keep their longhouses, crafts and dances alive.

name: Fort Margherita
description: An English-style fort built in 1879 by the White Rajahs to guard Kuching, now overlooking the river from its north bank.

name: Niah Caves
description: Giant caverns where 40,000-year-old human remains were found — among the oldest evidence of people in Southeast Asia.

name: Kellie's Castle
description: The unfinished Moorish-Scottish mansion of planter William Kellie Smith, wrapped in legends of hidden tunnels and ghosts.

name: Ubudiah Mosque
description: The golden-domed royal mosque of Kuala Kangsar, often called Malaysia's most beautiful, completed in 1917.

name: Crystal Mosque
description: A steel-and-glass mosque on Wan Man Island that appears to float on the Terengganu River, glowing in changing colours at night.

name: Sultan Abu Bakar State Mosque
description: A hilltop Victorian-Malay mosque overlooking the Straits of Johor, built between 1892 and 1900 for Sultan Abu Bakar.

name: Atkinson Clock Tower
description: Kota Kinabalu's oldest standing structure (1905), a wooden clock tower that survived the wartime destruction of the city.

name: Pinang Peranakan Mansion
description: A restored Baba-Nyonya townhouse packed with antiques, showing the opulent world of the Straits Chinese in George Town.
